data.narrative:
Telesat Lightspeed is a publicly traded space and aerospace company that has raised C$2.1B to date under CEO Dan Goldberg. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of four same-sector, same-stage public companies: Rocket Lab ($37.0B), Planet Labs ($7.0B), Firefly Aerospace ($6.3B), and Astroscale ($900M).

Peer-median triangulation is the applicable method here, as the target's revenue is not disclosed and therefore a public-comp P/S multiple cannot be applied. The Space & Aerospace public comp universe shows a P/S median of 49.8x across six tickers, but this cannot be used without the target's revenue figure. The peer-median approach across the four same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $6.7B, with a range spanning $900M to $37.0B reflecting the wide dispersion in the peer set.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $5.7B to $7.6B, centered on the peer median of $6.7B. Key uncertainties include the wide spread within the comparable set—driven substantially by Rocket Lab's outsized $37.0B valuation—and the absence of revenue or profitability metrics to refine positioning within that peer band. Sensitivity to which comparables are weighted most heavily remains a material driver of final valuation conclusions.
